rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute complete oesophageal food-bolus impaction with inability to clear saliva (active drooling/regurgitation and aspiration risk) is an endoscopic emergency requiring Emergency Department attendance now for urgent disimpaction — T4 (emergency same-day in-person/ED care). It is not baseline T5 because the airway is currently patent (normal voice, no stridor, SpO2 99%) and there is no current shock or perforation; however it sits immediately below a strong T5 edge, and an ambulance (000) is a reasonable transport choice given the aspiration risk. Under-triaging to GP or home advice would be a critical error, as would the commission error of advising fluids to force the bolus down.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Cannot swallow saliva / drooling and regurgitating secretions (present now) — complete obstruction with aspiration risk
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Get to the Emergency Department now for endoscopic removal; do not wait for clinic hours
escalation_edges.rationale: Complete obstruction will not reliably self-resolve and retained secretions threaten the airway; emergency endoscopy is the definitive treatment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because you can't even swallow your own saliva, this is a complete blockage that needs to be cleared in hospital today with a camera. Please go to the Emergency Department now — do not eat or drink anything, keep sitting up and spit your saliva into the cup.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe or worsening retrosternal chest pain, or pain that becomes tearing and radiates through to the back
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately if the pain becomes severe/tearing or radiates to the back
escalation_edges.rationale: Tearing pain radiating to the back can signal oesophageal perforation, a surgical emergency with high mortality if delayed; a cardiac cause of chest pain must also be considered.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the chest pain suddenly becomes severe or tearing, or shoots through to your back, call 000 straight away — that can mean a tear in the gullet and needs emergency treatment.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New harsh/barking cough, difficulty breathing, noisy breathing or a change in voice, or collapse
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately and do not wait
escalation_edges.rationale: These signal aspiration or airway compromise (or, with fever/shock, perforation with sepsis) — an immediate threat to life requiring an emergency ambulance.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he starts coughing harshly, his breathing becomes difficult or noisy, his voice changes, or he collapses, call 000 for an ambulance immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Partner is present and made the call and can arrange transport and monitor breathing
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Enlist the partner to get him to the Emergency Department now, to watch for any breathing difficulty or voice change during transport, and to call 000 immediately if that happens rather than continuing to drive.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Go to the Emergency Department now — this is a complete food blockage in the gullet that needs to be removed today with a camera (endoscopy). Do not eat or drink anything at all, including water, and do not try to wash it down with fizzy drink. Stay sitting upright and keep spitting your saliva into a cup instead of swallowing it. Given you cannot swallow, having your partner drive you straight to ED (or calling an ambulance) is the safest plan.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Attend the Emergency Department now; call 000 for an ambulance if breathing difficulty, voice change, or severe tearing back pain develops
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: This cannot wait for clinic hours — the Emergency Department and 000 are available 24 hours a day and this is an after-hours emergency.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide an endoscopy report and biopsy results — take these to the GP and the gastroenterology follow-up so the underlying cause can be treated and further impactions prevented.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating an acute complete oesophageal impaction as a routine swallowing/reflux problem and advising home care or GP review in the next few days.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Ongoing aspiration of pooled secretions causing aspiration pneumonia, or prolonged impaction leading to pressure necrosis and oesophageal perforation.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Defaulting to a 000 ambulance as the only framing while the airway is patent is acceptable (aspiration risk) and not scored as harmful over-triage; it becomes over-management only if it delays getting the patient to endoscopy.
